title: Food Stamps/SNAP * Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Food > Food Stamps/SNAP * Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions 50 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Domestic Violence Issues Older Adults Veterans No Filter Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness 211INFO 211INFO SNAP AND FOOD ACCESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The 211info SNAP and Food Access program provides specialized information, referral, advocacy, follow-up services, and extra guidance to individuals and family members who would like to access the SNAP program, have a question or issue with their current SNAP case, and/or need extra guidance accessing food resources. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details LANE COUNCIL OF GOVERNMENTS SENIOR & DISABILITY SERVICES EUGENE OFFICE SNAP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides administration of SNAP (food stamps). 1015 Willamette Street Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Age 60 and older and adults with disabilities age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY AGING DISABILITY AND VETERANS SERVICES DIVISION CENTRAL OFFICE SNAP, MEDICAID, AND TRANSPORTATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services to older adults, adults with disabilities, veterans, and caregivers. Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES SELF SUFFICIENCY ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE PRINTABLE SNAP APPLICATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a paper application for food benefits (SNAP), cash benefits (TANF), or child care benefits (ERDC) for people who do not want to apply online or by phone. 500 Summer Street NE E48 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Eligibility is determined on a case-by-case basis: -Income eligibility follows a guideline of 200% federal poverty level or below which is monthly income before taxes. -Other factors are considered in determining eligibility and monthly SNAP benefit amount like: source of income, housing costs, utility costs, out-of-pocket medical expenses for those age 60 and older or receiving disability in the group, and relationships of the members of the family group. -Students, immigrants, and adults between the ages of 18 and 52 without dependents may have to meet additional criteria beyond the income guidelines. -To receive SNAP, applicants must be a US citizen or meet specific non-citizen requirements.
